activities_projects.title: Family Traits Survey
steps: Survey at least 10-15 family members or relatives
Record traits like tongue rolling, attached/free earlobes, dimples, eye color, hair type
Tabulate who has dominant vs. recessive traits
Draw a simple family tree showing pattern
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes how traits are inherited from parents
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of data and pattern identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mendel's Pea Plant Model
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate monohybrid cross and laws of inheritance
steps: Choose one trait (e.g., tall/short plants)
Prepare Punnett square for F1 and F2 generations
Use colored beans to simulate crossing and show ratios (3:1)
Display on chart with explanation of dominance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands Mendel's laws and segregation of alleles
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness of ratios and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Inheritance of Blood Groups Poster
activities_projects.objective: Learn about multiple alleles and codominance
steps: Research ABO blood groups and Rh factor
Illustrate possible blood groups from parents (e.g., A and B parents)
Show Punnett squares and real-life importance (transfusions)
Add impact on health/population
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates complex inheritance patterns beyond simple dominance

activities_projects.title: Sex Determination Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand role of chromosomes in sex determination
steps: Draw human chromosomes (46 total, focus on pair 23)
Show XX (female) and XY (male) gametes
Prepare Punnett square for sex determination (50:50 ratio)
Discuss exceptions or sex-linked traits briefly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns chromosomal basis of sex determination
activities_projects.evaluation: Diagram clarity and conceptual accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Variation in Population Activity
steps: Measure height/hand span of 20-30 students
Record data and plot a simple bar graph/histogram
Discuss why variations exist (sexual reproduction, mutations)
Relate to chapter concept of variations being useful for species
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands role of variation in evolution/survival
activities_projects.evaluation: Data collection, graph, and discussion quality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Pedigree Analysis of Traits
steps: Choose a trait (e.g., color blindness if known, or curly hair)
Create a pedigree chart for 3 generations
Identify if autosomal dominant/recessive or sex-linked
Present and explain findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies inheritance concepts to real family data

activities_projects.title: Genetic Terms Crossword Puzzle
steps: List 10-12 terms (e.g., allele, dominant, recessive, genotype, phenotype, heredity, gene, chromosome)
Create a crossword or word search puzzle
Exchange with peers to solve and discuss meanings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Strengthens scientific terminology related to heredity

activities_projects.title: Role Play: Mendel’s Experiments
steps: Group members act as Mendel performing crosses
Explain pure breeding, hybridization, F1/F2 results
Highlight observations leading to laws of inheritance
Perform short skit for class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Deepens understanding of historical scientific process
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of science and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Quiz on Heredity Concepts
steps: Prepare 15-20 questions (MCQs, short answer on traits, crosses, sex determination)
Conduct group quiz competition
Discuss answers and clarify doubts

activities_projects.title: Debate: Role of Heredity vs Environment
steps: Divide class into teams
Debate using examples (intelligence, height, diseases)
Relate to chapter (inherited traits vs acquired variations)
Conclude with balanced view
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking on inheritance factors
